question: What are the main subassemblies of the RVI (Reactor Vessel Internals)?

nuscale_ch01_0135.title: Table 1.9-3: Conformance with NUREG-0800, Standard Review Plan (SRP) and Design Specific Review Standard (DSRS)
nuscale_ch01_0135.summary: Table 1.9-3 summarizes NuScale’s conformance with the regulatory criteria outlined in NUREG-0800 (SRP) and DSRS. It details which sections are applicable, whether NuScale conforms to each criterion, and provides comments or notes on applicability, particularly distinguishing requirements for COL applicants versus the NuScale design certification application.

nuscale_ch01_0005.title: Reactor Type
nuscale_ch01_0005.summary: The NuScale reactor is a small modular pressurized water reactor (PWR) featuring a cylindrical steel containment vessel designed to ASME standards, which operates under vacuum during normal conditions and uses natural circulation for coolant flow, eliminating the need for pumps. Each NuScale Power Module (NPM) produces 160 MWt (about 50 MWe), and a plant can have up to 12 modules for a total net output of approximately 570 MWe, with the design emphasizing passive safety and modular scalability.

nuscale_ch01_0073.title: Departures
nuscale_ch01_0073.summary: This section outlines the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant’s certified design and the site-specific design provided in the Combined License (COL) application. It distinguishes between conceptual design information (CDI) for non-certified portions and COL information items, which are site-specific responsibilities. Additionally, it requires COL applicants to provide a list of any departures from the certified design.

edges.description: Section A lists regulatory conformance criteria for the NuScale design, while Section B provides a general description of the containment vessel type, with both sections connected by their reference to the NuScale reactor's design features and regulatory compliance.

Section A identifies the structures, systems, and components included in the certified design, while Section B documents the conformance of those components (as referenced in Section A) with regulatory criteria such as the SRP and DSRS.

Section A outlines the interface requirements and identifies structures, systems, and components included in the certified design, which provides context for understanding the containment vessel described in Section B as part of those components.
